title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Static Boundary
summary: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the static boundary increment in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_outcomes_repo_architecture_3(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Static Boundary evidence · Theory Static Boundary
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-static-boundary-1v8doh" that uses case 7525 to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Static Boundary evidence · Theory Static Boundary.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Static Boundary into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Static Boundary evidence · Theory Static Boundary, keep Static Boundary connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-static-boundary-1v8doh
# Competency: ssg-static-boundary.
# Case 7525: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_static_boundary_1v8doh(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1v8doh = "1v8doh-hod8v1-7525"
    assert evidence_variant_1v8doh
    evidence_axes_1v8doh = (
        "reader1v8doh",
        "source7525",
        "parsehod8v1",
        "route1v8doh7525",
        "artifact75251v8doh",
        "recoveryhod8v17525",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1v8doh)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Static Boundary model · Theory Static Boundary
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Static Boundary: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 9357 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish build-time transformation, immutable output, browser execution, origin hosting, CDN behavior, and external services.
